item.title: ‘AI, “Humanity”, and Dr. Manhattan Syndrome’ 摘要
item.description:
Jim Prosser draws a parallel between the communications failures of the nuclear power industry and those of AI leaders today. He argues that nuclear power's decline was caused by actual disasters, not just poor communication, but the industry's failure to build public trust left it vulnerable. Similarly, AI leaders like Greg Brockman address public anxiety about job loss, privacy, and creative work with abstract philosophical rhetoric rather than concrete human-scale concerns. Prosser warns that this disconnect risks repeating history, where the public's legitimate worries are dismissed, leading to a loss of trust and potential backlash against AI.
标签: AI, public trust, communication, nuclear power, technology ethics, risk perception, AI safety, public perception, history, technology criticism

item.title: Existing Stakeholders Have a Say in the Future 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber responds to Steven Levy's vision of AI agents replacing ride-hailing apps by arguing that entrenched stakeholders like Uber and Lyft will resist such disruption. He notes that these companies control their platforms and may not cede control to AI providers like OpenAI or Anthropic. Drawing a parallel to Walmart's refusal to accept Apple Pay, Gruber emphasizes that existing players often slow technological adoption rather than passively being replaced. He concludes that assuming all companies will embrace AI-driven changes is naive.
标签: AI, technology, business, Uber, Lyft, Apple Pay, disruption, stakeholders, hype, platforms

item.title: Ted Turner’s Small Apartment Above the Former CNN Center 摘要
item.description:
Ted Turner, the media mogul who founded CNN, is remembered for his audacious yet humble nature, epitomized by his small apartment above the former CNN Center. This living arrangement, reminiscent of Walt Disney's apartment above the fire station at Disneyland, reflects Turner's unique character and legacy following his recent death.
标签: Ted Turner, CNN, media, obituary, humble living, legacy, Walt Disney, Disneyland, entrepreneurship, history

item.title: Define ‘Boom’ Please 摘要
item.description:
John Gruber critiques a New York Times article claiming Apple missed the AI boom. He argues that while Nvidia benefits from AI hardware sales, Apple doesn't compete with Nvidia and has avoided funneling cash into AI datacenters. Gruber contends that calling AI a 'boom' for Apple's peers is misleading, as AI spending is currently a money pit rather than a proven profit source. Apple's profits and stock remain strong despite not chasing AI infrastructure.
标签: Apple, AI, Tim Cook, Nvidia, datacenter, profits, stock, tech industry, analysis

item.title: ‘John Appleseed’ 摘要
item.description:
Tim Cook's leadership at Apple since 2011 saw market cap rise from $350 billion to $4 trillion, revenue quadruple to $416 billion, and Services grow into a $100 billion business. While Cook inherited a strong product portfolio, he maintained operational excellence through challenges like a pandemic and trade wars. Though not a product visionary like Steve Jobs, Cook's key achievement was not disrupting Apple's success. Services alone now matches Apple's entire size when Cook took over, highlighting the difficulty of sustaining success.
标签: Apple, Tim Cook, leadership, business, Services, market cap, revenue, operations, succession, tech industry

item.title: Jury Rejects Elon Musk’s Claim Against Sam Altman in Unanimous Verdict 摘要
item.description:
A unanimous jury rejected Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, ruling that the claim was filed after the three-year statute of limitations expired. Musk filed the suit in summer 2024, but the jury found he was aware of the alleged misconduct as early as 2021. Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers delivered poetic jury instructions, praising the jury as a temporary, impartial body that reflects community values and has no ambition.
标签: OpenAI, Elon Musk, Sam Altman, lawsuit, jury verdict, statute of limitations, artificial intelligence, legal, New York Times, Daring Fireball

item.title: Something’s Rotten in the State of macOS Icon Design 摘要
item.description:
The article critiques the decline in macOS icon design quality, noting that Apple's recent icons, when viewed in reverse chronological order, appear to regress in creativity and detail. The author argues that Apple's restrictive design guidelines, particularly the squircle shape, have homogenized icons across the platform, making even third-party apps like BBEdit and Fantastical look less distinctive. While some designers still produce excellent work, Apple's own icons set a poor standard, limiting the entire ecosystem's visual potential.
标签: macOS, icon design, Apple, UI design, design trends, software aesthetics, Apple guidelines, third-party apps, visual regression

item.title: 10Gb/s Ethernet: using mini-heatsinks with a 10GBASE-T SFP+ module 摘要
item.description:
The author tested mini-heatsinks on a MikroTik 10GBASE-T SFP+ module to reduce high temperatures. After attaching two heatsinks, the module's temperature dropped by about 3.5°C over 24 hours, a modest improvement. The post also discusses two generations of SFP+ modules: older ones (like the MikroTik S+RJ10) using Marvell chips rated for 30 meters, and newer ones using Broadcom chips rated for 100 meters. The author considers switching to newer modules but will wait unless summer heat causes issues.
标签: networking, 10GbE, SFP+, heatsink, temperature, MikroTik, hardware, cooling, homelab

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github 摘要
item.description:
A CISA contractor exposed highly sensitive credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository named 'Private-CISA' from November 2025 until mid-May 2026. The leak included AWS GovCloud admin keys, plaintext passwords for dozens of internal systems, and code repository access. Security researchers from GitGuardian and Seralys discovered the exposure, noting that the contractor had disabled GitHub's secret detection feature. CISA acknowledged the incident but claimed no data compromise occurred. The repository was taken down, but exposed AWS keys remained valid for 48 hours afterward.
标签: CISA, data leak, GitHub, AWS GovCloud, credentials exposure, government security, contractor, security breach, cloud security, DevSecOps
